title: quercus fruticosa var. vulgaris
intro: Quercus fruticosa var. vulgaris, also known as the Korean oak, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its small leaves and short trunk. The medicinal properties of Quercus fruticosa var. vulgaris include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an anti-cancer agent, as well as providing antimicrobial and anti-viral properties. Quercus fruticosa var. vulgaris can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also used in traditional Korean medicine as a powder or extract.
